differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Depressive symptoms are common across multiple disorders. A symptom label is not the same as an episode-level diagnosis.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When episode threshold, bipolar history, medical contributors, or substance timeline are incomplete.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When the note only supports depressive symptoms, sad mood, or stress response without a defined major depressive episode.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Inpatient teams may use working diagnoses quickly for care coordination; outpatient longitudinal review should still stay conservative about bipolar and substance exclusions.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Mania is often overcalled from irritability, insomnia, or activation without sustained syndrome-level change.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When activation is brief, occurs only during intoxication or antidepressant exposure, or lacks clear functional change.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When the record shows mood lability or 'possible mania' rather than a bona fide manic episode.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: In inpatient or ED settings, acute severity may tempt early labeling; outpatient longitudinal history often reveals alternative explanations.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Bipolar II is frequently overdiagnosed from recurrent depression plus short bursts of energy or irritability.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When hypomania duration, observable change, and episode recurrence are not documented.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When 'bipolar depression' appears in chart language without clear hypomanic history.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Outpatient psychotherapy or medication notes may inherit the label without full verification; review should be stricter there.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Cyclothymia requires a chronic fluctuating pattern, not just emotional reactivity.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When the note only shows general mood instability or interpersonal reactivity.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When clinicians use 'cyclothymic traits' loosely or as shorthand for mood swings.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Outpatient notes are more prone to trait-language creep; inpatient short stays rarely support the necessary chronicity.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Psychosis language is often inferential, especially when based on behavior like RTIS or guardedness.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When hallucinations/delusions are not directly elicited or when medical/substance causes remain plausible.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When the source only supports bizarre behavior, paranoia, or disorganization without psychotic criteria clearly documented.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Inpatient settings may use provisional psychosis labels more often; outpatient systems should preserve 'rule out psychotic disorder' unless the timeline and symptoms are clearer.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Schizophrenia is often used colloquially to mean psychosis, but the actual diagnosis requires longitudinal duration and exclusion review.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When there is only first-episode psychosis, unclear substance timeline, or incomplete six-month course.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When the note shows only acute psychosis or inherited chart history without confirming chronology.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Inpatient teams may document working schizophrenia-spectrum impressions; outpatient confirmation should usually be more conservative.

commonly_confused_alternatives: major depressive disorder with psychotic features
differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: This diagnosis hinges on longitudinal separation of psychosis from mood episodes and is famously easy to misuse.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When psychosis is documented only during mood episodes or when total illness timeline is not reviewed.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When the chart merely says 'schizoaffective' or 'SZA' without explaining the period of psychosis independent of mood symptoms.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Both inpatient and outpatient notes can inherit this label from history; automatic strengthening should be avoided in all settings.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Trauma exposure alone does not equal PTSD, and early post-trauma symptoms may not meet the duration threshold.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When trauma exposure is implied but not explicit, symptom clusters are incomplete, or symptoms are still within the first month.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When the note only supports trauma history, nightmares, hypervigilance, or anxiety after stress without a full PTSD picture.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Outpatient therapy notes may use trauma-focused language broadly; diagnosis automation should stay conservative.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Adjustment disorder is often used as a residual diagnosis when the differential is unresolved.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When the stressor is not identified, timing is unclear, or a more specific disorder may be met instead.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When clinicians use 'situational depression/anxiety' loosely.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Outpatient settings often inherit this label from initial crises; later notes should reevaluate whether another diagnosis fits better.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Worry is nonspecific; GAD requires chronicity and breadth, not simply 'anxious today.'
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When anxiety is tied to a discrete stressor, trauma, panic, substances, or medical symptoms.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When chart language says 'anxious' or 'significant worry' without a 6-month pattern.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Outpatient notes are especially prone to silently upgrading anxious symptoms to GAD for medication continuity.

commonly_confused_alternatives: anxiety disorder due to medical condition
differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Panic attacks occur in many disorders and are not equivalent to panic disorder.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When there is only one attack, a clear medical trigger, or no persistent post-attack worry/behavior change.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When notes document 'panic attacks' but not the longitudinal disorder.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: ED or urgent settings often capture attacks; outpatient systems should not auto-convert attacks into panic disorder.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Everyday use of 'obsessive' and 'compulsive' creates false positives.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When intrusive thoughts are not ego-dystonic, compulsions are absent, or perfectionism/rigidity is the better fit.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When the note says the patient is obsessive, perfectionistic, or ruminative without core OCD phenomena.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Outpatient therapy notes often describe traits; diagnosis suggestions should remain review-only.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Concentration complaints are among the most nonspecific symptoms in mental health.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When developmental onset, cross-setting presence, and persistence are undocumented.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When the source only supports poor focus, procrastination, executive dysfunction, or distractibility.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Adult outpatient notes are particularly vulnerable to symptom-to-diagnosis upgrades; inpatient settings rarely establish ADHD de novo.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: ASD is developmental and requires more than social awkwardness or sensory preferences.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When clinicians document autistic traits without developmental history or functional pattern across settings.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When notes say 'possibly autistic' or use legacy Asperger terminology casually.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Outpatient psychotherapy language about neurodivergence should not be silently hardened into ASD diagnosis logic.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: BPD is frequently confused with bipolar disorder and is often documented with stigmatizing shorthand.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When only mood lability, self-harm history, or interpersonal conflict is documented without persistent personality pattern evidence.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When the note uses pejorative trait language or implies manipulativeness instead of describing patterns.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Inpatient crisis notes may over-ascribe acute dysregulation to BPD; outpatient longitudinal observation is more important.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Behavioral history alone does not automatically establish a personality disorder, and conduct history matters.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When the chart only documents aggression, legal problems, or 'sociopathic' behavior.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When clinicians use sociopath or antisocial colloquially rather than diagnostically.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: In correctional or emergency settings, shorthand can overstate certainty; outpatient systems should be conservative.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: The key issue is timing relative to intoxication, withdrawal, or medication exposure, which many notes do not structure clearly.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When there is no clear substance timeline or sustained abstinence data.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When the note merely documents substance use plus depression without stating temporal linkage.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: In inpatient detox or consultation settings, provisional substance-induced impressions are common; outpatient follow-up should revisit persistence after abstinence.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Psychosis around intoxication or withdrawal may resolve, but the timeline is often messy.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When onset relative to substance exposure is unclear or psychosis persists well beyond expected intoxication/withdrawal windows.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When 'meth psychosis' or 'drug psychosis' is used without a documented temporal sequence.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Emergency and inpatient settings see these presentations first; outpatient systems should avoid prematurely making them primary psychotic disorders.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Delirium is often missed if acute confusion is framed as psychiatric by default.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When onset, fluctuation, attention deficits, or medical contributors are incompletely documented.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When the note supports 'altered', confusion, or agitation but not a stable primary psychiatric syndrome.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: In medical and inpatient settings, delirium should stay high on the differential; outpatient records usually lack the bedside data to confirm it.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Cognitive complaints are common, but severity hinges on functional impact and progressive decline.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When objective testing, collateral, or functional status are missing.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When the note only says memory issues, confusion, or forgetfulness.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Outpatient longitudinal follow-up is often needed; inpatient acute illness can transiently worsen cognition.

differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: DMDD was developed partly to avoid overdiagnosing bipolar disorder in chronically irritable youth.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When the note only supports irritability or tantrums without developmental and cross-setting review.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When child behavior is described in broad terms without syndrome-level patterning.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Pediatric outpatient longitudinal data are especially important; single crisis encounters are weak evidence.

commonly_confused_alternatives: sleep apnea or other sleep-wake disorders
differential_cautions.what_makes_documentation_risky: Poor sleep is a symptom across psychiatry and medicine, not automatically a sleep disorder diagnosis.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_preserve_uncertainty: When frequency, duration, daytime impact, or adequate sleep opportunity are not documented.
differential_cautions.when_app_should_avoid_upgrading_symptoms: When notes say insomnia but only describe transient or symptom-level sleep difficulty.
differential_cautions.inpatient_vs_outpatient_certainty_differ: Outpatient medication follow-ups often inherit insomnia as a diagnosis even when it is secondary to another active condition.

specifier_library.meaning: Indicates one identified episode to date.
commonly_applies_to: major depressive disorder and other episodic disorders
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Unsafe if prior episodes are merely undocumented rather than absent.

specifier_library.meaning: Indicates more than one episode over time.
commonly_applies_to: major depressive disorder and several episodic disorders
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Requires reliable longitudinal history; copying it forward can overstate certainty.

specifier_library.meaning: Severity grading based on symptom burden, impairment, and sometimes psychosis or suicidality.
commonly_applies_to: depressive, anxiety, eating, and substance-related disorders
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Often inferred casually without explicit rationale; severity language is easily inflated.

specifier_library.meaning: Psychotic symptoms occur in the context of a mood disorder episode.
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Critical differential with schizoaffective disorder and schizophrenia-spectrum diagnoses.

specifier_library.meaning: Anxiety symptoms are prominent alongside another primary diagnosis.
commonly_applies_to: mood disorders and some other conditions
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Can be useful clinically, but not all notes support the level of specificity needed.

specifier_library.meaning: Symptoms from the opposite pole accompany a mood episode.
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Commonly overused when agitation or irritability are present but mixed syndrome is not established.

specifier_library.meaning: Catatonic syndrome accompanies another psychiatric or medical condition.
specifier_library.documentation_risk: High-acuity term that should not be suggested from vague motor or withdrawal descriptions alone.

specifier_library.meaning: Depersonalization or derealization features occur with PTSD.
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Should not be inferred from generic emotional numbing or detachment language.

specifier_library.meaning: The full syndrome becomes diagnosable only after an interval following the precipitating event.
commonly_applies_to: PTSD and some trauma-related documentation contexts
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Requires timeline evidence; easy to misuse when symptom onset details are sparse.

specifier_library.meaning: Some clinically relevant symptoms persist, but full syndrome is no longer met.
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Can overstate stability if current symptoms are still functionally significant.

specifier_library.meaning: Full syndrome is no longer met and symptoms are absent or minimal by current assessment.
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Risky when based on a single visit or when residual symptoms remain material.

specifier_library.meaning: Symptoms are judged to result from a substance, withdrawal, or medication rather than a purely primary psychiatric disorder.
commonly_applies_to: mood, anxiety-like, sleep, and psychotic presentations
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Requires a temporal relationship and is unsafe to infer without timeline data.

specifier_library.meaning: Mental symptoms are attributed primarily to a medical condition.
commonly_applies_to: psychosis, mood, anxiety, sleep, and cognitive presentations
specifier_library.documentation_risk: High-risk inference that should not be suggested without strong clinical evidence.

specifier_library.meaning: A more specific diagnosis is not used, but the clinician chooses to document why the presentation does not meet full criteria.
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Useful for honesty, but the rationale should be visible rather than hidden by UI.

specifier_library.meaning: The clinician does not specify why full criteria are not met or enough information is unavailable.
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Safe as a placeholder, but should not silently become a more specific diagnosis later.

specifier_library.meaning: A diagnosis is suspected but not yet fully confirmed because more observation or information is needed.
commonly_applies_to: psychotic, neurodevelopmental, and other longitudinal diagnoses
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Especially important for duration-sensitive diagnoses such as schizophreniform disorder.

specifier_library.meaning: Subtype qualifier used with schizoaffective disorder to describe the predominant mood-episode pattern.
specifier_library.documentation_risk: Unsafe if the underlying schizoaffective diagnosis itself is uncertain.

icd_linkage.label: Depressive disorders
icd_linkage.likely_icd10_cm_family_linkage: F32.x-F39, commonly F32.x/F33.x for major depressive disorder family; unspecified depressive conditions may fall elsewhere in the F3x range
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Episode status, recurrence, severity, psychotic features, and remission can affect code specificity.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Do not assume recurrent vs single episode or psychotic features from casual chart language.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: Offer depressive-family suggestions before exact code-like labels unless specifiers are confirmed.

icd_linkage.label: Bipolar disorders
icd_linkage.diagnosis_or_family: bipolar I, bipolar II, cyclothymia, bipolar-related disorders
icd_linkage.likely_icd10_cm_family_linkage: F30-F31 series for mania/bipolar-related conditions; cyclothymic disorder is commonly F34.0
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Current episode type and remission status drive specificity; bipolar I vs II should not be conflated.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Symptoms like mood lability or 'bipolar depression' do not identify the exact family member.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: UI should ask for episode polarity and verified diagnosis before surfacing specific bipolar codes.

icd_linkage.label: Psychotic disorders
icd_linkage.diagnosis_or_family: schizophrenia spectrum and other psychotic disorders
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Duration-sensitive distinctions such as brief psychotic disorder, schizophreniform disorder, schizophrenia, delusional disorder, and unspecified psychosis require different code families within F20-F29.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Single-visit notes often do not support the exact longitudinal subtype.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: Default to psychosis-spectrum family suggestions and require review for subtype finalization.

icd_linkage.label: Schizoaffective disorder
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Type qualifiers and supporting longitudinal data matter.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Schizoaffective is often inherited as a historical label without renewed evidence.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: UI should show it as high-review, not as a low-friction autocomplete.

icd_linkage.label: Anxiety disorders
icd_linkage.diagnosis_or_family: GAD, panic disorder, social anxiety disorder, phobias, unspecified anxiety
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Different anxiety disorders live in adjacent but distinct families; panic attacks alone do not determine panic disorder coding.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Brief anxious symptoms, stress, or trauma-related anxiety should not auto-code as GAD or panic disorder.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: Start at family level and require duration/context questions before specificity.

icd_linkage.label: Obsessive-compulsive and related disorders
icd_linkage.diagnosis_or_family: OCD, body dysmorphic disorder, hoarding disorder, trichotillomania
icd_linkage.likely_icd10_cm_family_linkage: Commonly F42.x and F63.3/F63.81-adjacent or related F4x/F6x placements depending on condition
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Terminology is precise; perfectionism or intrusive thoughts alone do not determine OCD-family coding.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Casual chart wording about obsessions and compulsions is highly ambiguous.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: Offer review-only suggestions unless the condition is explicitly documented.

icd_linkage.label: Trauma- and stressor-related disorders
icd_linkage.diagnosis_or_family: PTSD, acute stress disorder, adjustment disorder
icd_linkage.likely_icd10_cm_family_linkage: PTSD typically F43.1x, acute stress and adjustment disorder in F43.x family
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Timing relative to trauma/stressor matters for choosing among PTSD, acute stress disorder, and adjustment disorder.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Trauma history alone is insufficient for specific code suggestions.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: UI should collect event timing and symptom duration before mapping beyond the F43 family.

icd_linkage.label: Dissociative disorders
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Legacy colloquial language like 'multiple personalities' is not coding-ready.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Many notes describe dissociation phenomenologically without enough specificity for a single diagnosis.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: Keep family suggestions conservative and preserve the original descriptive language.

icd_linkage.label: Somatic symptom and related disorders
icd_linkage.diagnosis_or_family: somatic symptom disorder, illness anxiety disorder, functional neurologic disorder
icd_linkage.likely_icd10_cm_family_linkage: Often F45.x and related family placements
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: The family includes distinct disorders that are easy to conflate when charts use words like psychosomatic or hypochondriasis.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Physical symptoms may still warrant medical evaluation; psychiatric coding certainty should stay cautious.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: Prefer descriptive suggestions plus review rather than hard mapping from slang or legacy labels.

icd_linkage.label: Eating disorders
icd_linkage.diagnosis_or_family: anorexia nervosa, bulimia nervosa, binge-eating disorder, ARFID
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Subtype and severity details matter; binge eating and ARFID should not be inferred from appetite complaints alone.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Weight/appetite documentation can be nonspecific in psychiatric notes.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: UI should separate appetite symptoms from eating disorder diagnoses.

icd_linkage.label: Sleep-wake disorders
icd_linkage.diagnosis_or_family: insomnia disorder and related sleep diagnoses
icd_linkage.likely_icd10_cm_family_linkage: Commonly G47.x for many sleep disorders; psychiatric notes may still mention insomnia symptomatically without a final sleep-disorder code
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Insomnia can be primary, comorbid, or symptom-level rather than a standalone coded disorder.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Poor sleep alone is too weak for automatic coding.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: UI should distinguish symptom tracking from formal sleep-disorder diagnosis entry.

icd_linkage.label: Disruptive, impulse-control, and conduct disorders
icd_linkage.likely_icd10_cm_family_linkage: F91.x and related F63/F34 placements depending on specific disorder
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Age, developmental context, and cross-setting behavior matter.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Tantrums, aggression, or irritability alone are insufficient for a final code.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: Make behavioral-disorder suggestions review-only and age-aware.

icd_linkage.label: Substance-related and addictive disorders
icd_linkage.diagnosis_or_family: AUD, OUD, stimulant use disorder, cannabis use disorder, gambling disorder
icd_linkage.likely_icd10_cm_family_linkage: F10-F19 families for substance-related diagnoses; gambling disorder is not in the F1x substance block
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Substance class, severity, intoxication/withdrawal states, remission, and induced disorders change specificity.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Products easily confuse use, intoxication, withdrawal, and use disorder.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: UI should collect substance class and current state separately before suggesting a final diagnosis.

icd_linkage.label: Substance-induced conditions
icd_linkage.diagnosis_or_family: substance/medication-induced mood, anxiety-like, sleep, or psychotic disorders
icd_linkage.likely_icd10_cm_family_linkage: Scattered across the relevant psychiatric chapters and specific substance families
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Requires linking symptom syndrome to exposure state, not just knowing the primary symptom.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Timeline uncertainty makes exact coding especially unsafe.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: Use a dedicated timeline UI and prevent one-click conversion from substance use mention to substance-induced diagnosis.

icd_linkage.label: Neurodevelopmental disorders
icd_linkage.diagnosis_or_family: ADHD, ASD, intellectual disability, learning disorder
icd_linkage.likely_icd10_cm_family_linkage: F70-F89 family depending on the specific disorder
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Developmental history and specifiers matter; older legacy labels may not map cleanly to current terminology.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Adult symptom descriptions often omit childhood onset and cross-setting evidence.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: UI should support legacy-history capture separately from current normalized taxonomy.

icd_linkage.label: Personality disorders
icd_linkage.diagnosis_or_family: borderline, antisocial, avoidant, narcissistic, unspecified personality disorder
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Personality diagnosis often requires longitudinal pattern evidence that many notes do not summarize explicitly.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Trait language and pejorative wording can masquerade as diagnosis language.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: UI should separate trait descriptors from formal personality-disorder selection.

icd_linkage.label: Neurocognitive disorders
icd_linkage.diagnosis_or_family: mild and major neurocognitive disorders, delirium, Alzheimer-related conditions
icd_linkage.likely_icd10_cm_family_linkage: F01-F03, G30-related combinations, and delirium codes depending on etiology and setting
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Etiology and severity drive specificity; cognitive complaints alone are not code-ready.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Depression, delirium, or MCI can be confused with dementia.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: UI should ask whether the clinician is documenting symptom, syndrome, or specific etiology.

icd_linkage.label: Unspecified / other specified
icd_linkage.specificity_issues: Unspecified options are sometimes the only honest choice when information is incomplete.
icd_linkage.uncertainty_issues: Autosuggest systems often push users away from uncertainty and toward false specificity.
icd_linkage.product_implications_for_diagnosis_sugge: Make unspecified and other specified easy to pick, with rationale prompts when appropriate.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Common chart phrase, but it can obscure whether the clinician means bipolar I, bipolar II, current depressive episode, or a historical label.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Keep as provider text or map only after a verified bipolar diagnosis exists.

terms_to_avoid.term_or_phrase: manic / mania used for irritability alone
terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Irritability is nonspecific and can reflect anxiety, trauma, personality structure, substance effects, sleep loss, or depression.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Describe activation, sleep change, goal-directed behavior, and duration rather than upgrading to mania automatically.

terms_to_avoid.term_or_phrase: psychotic because RTIS / talking to self / staring
terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Observed behavior can suggest internal preoccupation but does not prove hallucinations or a psychotic disorder.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Retain as observed behavior or internal preoccupation unless psychotic symptoms are directly supported.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Often overstates certainty when only acute psychosis or brief duration is documented.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Use psychosis, unspecified psychotic disorder, or provisional schizophrenia-spectrum language pending longitudinal data.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: This diagnosis has specific longitudinal requirements and is often inherited in charts without renewed verification.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Keep history as quoted history unless current documentation supports the longitudinal distinction.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: May refer either to a specific diagnosis or to a dismissive characterization of behavior.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Use the formal diagnosis only when supported; otherwise describe observed patterns nonjudgmentally.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Stigmatizing and interpretive rather than descriptive, especially in personality-disorder documentation.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Describe the behavior, request, or interpersonal pattern instead of assigning motive.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Blame-laden and often hides barriers such as side effects, access issues, ambivalence, or misunderstanding.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Use not taking medication as prescribed, missed doses, declined treatment, or specific barrier language.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Stigmatizing and inconsistent with person-first SUD language guidance.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Use substance use disorder, unhealthy use, person with opioid use disorder, etc.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Value-laden language about drug testing can stigmatize patients.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Use positive / negative toxicology or tested positive / negative.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Legacy, pejorative term that blurs illness anxiety disorder and somatic symptom disorder.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Use illness anxiety disorder, somatic symptom disorder, or descriptive health-anxiety language as appropriate.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Legacy and stigmatizing phrasing for functional neurologic symptoms.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Use functional neurologic disorder or descriptive symptom language.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Legacy terminology for dissociative identity disorder; may also be used loosely or sensationally.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Use dissociative identity disorder only when clinically supported.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Not equivalent to a formal DSM-style diagnosis in ordinary charting and often used pejoratively.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Use antisocial personality disorder only when diagnostic evidence exists; otherwise describe behavior.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Legacy labels may appear in prior records, but modern ASD documentation should handle them as historical terms with care.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Preserve as legacy history if quoted, but normalize internally to autism spectrum disorder only with explicit review.

terms_to_avoid.term_or_phrase: psychosis NOS / depression NOS / anxiety NOS
terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Legacy NOS wording may map imperfectly to current unspecified/other specified usage.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Store as legacy wording and map cautiously to unspecified/other specified only with review.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Can falsely imply low risk or remission without specifying what is stable and over what interval.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Specify which symptoms are unchanged, improved, or currently absent.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: May overstate current status if residual symptoms or recent instability persist.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Use improving, below prior severity, or partial remission when the evidence is limited.

terms_to_avoid.term_or_phrase: delusional used for implausible or strongly held belief without proper assessment
terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Delusion is a high-certainty psychosis term and should not be used merely because a belief seems odd or is disputed.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Describe the belief and degree of conviction, or use possible delusional content pending assessment.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Can mean everyday mistrust, trauma-related vigilance, or persecutory delusions depending on context.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Preserve the original wording and prompt review rather than auto-map to delusional disorder or psychosis.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Executive dysfunction is nonspecific and may reflect mood, anxiety, trauma, sleep disorders, or substance use.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Require developmental onset and cross-setting persistence before suggesting ADHD.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Trauma exposure is not equivalent to PTSD and acute stress reactions may occur before the PTSD timeframe.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Use trauma history or trauma-related symptoms unless PTSD is supported.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: This diagnosis is often used when the differential is unresolved, which can hide more specific or more severe pathology.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Require identifiable stressor plus timing logic; otherwise preserve uncertainty.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Everyday forgetfulness, depression-related cognitive complaints, or delirium can be mislabeled as dementia.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Use cognitive complaint, possible neurocognitive disorder, or obtain collateral/testing before strengthening.

terms_to_avoid.why_risky: Diagnosis lists and note templates can propagate outdated or unsupported labels.
terms_to_avoid.safer_alternative_or_handling: Prompt explicit re-verification of high-risk diagnoses rather than silently carrying them forward.

product_design.purpose: Store official family/category structure and normalized diagnosis nodes used across search, UI grouping, and analytics.
product_design.provider_visible_or_internal_only: Both: provider-visible family labels; internal metadata fields hidden

product_design.purpose: Recognize chart shorthand, patient language, legacy labels, and misspellings while preserving ambiguity.
product_design.provider_visible_or_internal_only: Internal only, with optional provider-visible inline clarification

product_design.purpose: Encode minimum duration, episode structure, and timing constraints that separate closely related psychiatric diagnoses.
product_design.provider_visible_or_internal_only: Internal with optional provider-visible review prompts

product_design.purpose: Tell the system when multiple diagnoses are plausible and uncertainty should be preserved.
product_design.provider_visible_or_internal_only: Mostly internal, with provider-visible caution nudges for high-risk diagnoses

product_design.purpose: Store recurrence, severity, psychotic-features, remission, substance-induced, and unspecified/other specified modifiers.
product_design.safe_for_auto_suggestion_or_review_only: mixed; unspecified may be safer than false specificity, most others review_only

product_design.purpose: Provide practical code-family hints without overstating billable-code certainty.
product_design.provider_visible_or_internal_only: Both: visible family hints; internal caution logic

product_design.purpose: Flag wording that hardens symptoms, impressions, or legacy slang into more specific diagnoses than the note supports.
Store mappings from historical chart terms such as Asperger's, PDD-NOS, NOS, or quoted external diagnoses to modern internal normalization while preserving the source wording.
product_design.provider_visible_or_internal_only: Internal, with provider-visible history labels retained in the note

safe_to_build_directly_now: Diagnosis-family taxonomy and normalized diagnosis node IDs
